cross_cutting_themes.title: Shared contract leakage
cross_cutting_themes.underlying_issue: Both subsystems build richer internal contracts than they actually transport at runtime, leaving builder-layer/shared logic to diverge from adapter/native branches.
cross_cutting_themes.architectural_fix: Centralize shared builder/render/transport behavior behind one adapter contract per subsystem and make parity tests assert the structured contract rather than text-only or branch-local behavior.

cross_cutting_themes.title: Operator surfaces under-specify effective state
cross_cutting_themes.underlying_issue: Operator-facing tools and warnings omit the effective state they are acting on, including selected graph candidates, effective thresholds, workspace selection, and live-versus-default status.
cross_cutting_themes.architectural_fix: Promote effective state into first-class public schemas and status surfaces so operators can see selected candidates, effective thresholds, explicit workspace inputs, and live-vs-default markers.

cross_cutting_themes.title: Telemetry and feedback are not durable enough
cross_cutting_themes.underlying_issue: Useful internal signals exist, but they are not persisted and surfaced in ways that support durable health monitoring or learning from live behavior.
cross_cutting_themes.architectural_fix: Add durable, prompt-safe diagnostic sinks plus operator-facing health summaries, and make persisted signal lifecycle rules explicit so stale state can be cleared safely.

cross_cutting_themes.title: Tests still protect incidental behavior
cross_cutting_themes.underlying_issue: Several current tests encode accidental branch-local behavior or fail to assert the intended contract, allowing regressions to persist unnoticed.
cross_cutting_themes.architectural_fix: Rebuild regression coverage around desired contract outcomes and align docs/tests so transport, bridge-path, and ambiguity behavior all derive from the same source-of-truth.

findings.summary: Read-path handlers continue executing when ensure-ready requires a full scan but inline full scans are disabled, so callers can receive misleading empty or unresolved graph results.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce an explicit blocked-full-scan contract in query/context handlers and add tests for empty/full-scan-needed read paths.

findings.summary: The CocoIndex seed bridge throws away semantic score, snippet, and most range semantics before graph resolution, then reorders by local graph confidence.
findings.recommended_fix: Preserve CocoIndex score/snippet/range fidelity through seed resolution and blend semantic score with graph confidence when ordering resolved anchors.

findings.summary: Scan-time edge-enrichment metadata can remain stale because the scan handler does not clear the persisted summary when a later scan finds no enrichment evidence.
findings.recommended_fix: Allow null-summary scans to clear the stored enrichment summary and add regression coverage for overwrite-then-clear behavior.

findings.summary: Persisted detector-provenance and edge-enrichment summaries are still effectively write-only because status and startup readers do not consume them.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a graph-quality summary reader and surface it in code_graph_status and startup priming.

findings.summary: Startup structured graph payloads exist at the builder layer but are down-converted to text in runtime adapters, and adapter tests only guard text transport.
findings.recommended_fix: Either propagate a structured startup payload through every runtime adapter or remove the unused builder-level startup payload contract.

findings.summary: The code_graph_context bounded-work contract is only partial because the handler never sets deadlineMs, only impact mode enforces elapsed-time limits, and truncation is unlabeled in the response.
findings.recommended_fix: Set a real default deadline, enforce it across all modes, and add explicit partial-output metadata to context responses.

findings.summary: The zero-edge result came from querying the wrong node: the prior symbolId mapped to the lazy re-export in handlers/index.ts, while the real handleMemoryContext function in memory-context.ts currently has persisted CALLS edges.
findings.recommended_fix: Make subject resolution operation-aware for calls_from/calls_to so callable implementation nodes outrank export/import/wrapper nodes.

findings.summary: Ambiguity warnings expose only generic messaging plus symbol IDs, so operators cannot tell that a CALLS query selected a wrapper/export node instead of the real function.
findings.recommended_fix: Extend warning payloads and response metadata with candidate kind/file/line data plus the selected candidate.

findings.summary: The existing ambiguity regression test codifies deterministic first-candidate selection for calls_from, so the current suite does not protect against wrapper-node shadowing for CALLS queries.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a handleMemoryContext-style ambiguity test that seeds export/variable/function candidates and asserts that calls_from and calls_to pick the function candidate.

findings.summary: The bad-subject pattern is broad across the MCP surface because handlers/index.ts and similar index files create duplicate handle* names that shadow real implementation functions.
findings.recommended_fix: Apply the operation-aware resolver to all CALLS-oriented graph queries and verify it against sibling handle* names, not only handleMemoryContext.

findings.summary: OpenCode still has route-specific threshold drift because the native bridge path ignores configured thresholdConfidence while the fallback/shared path applies it.
findings.recommended_fix: Unify OpenCode threshold handling so both native and fallback bridge paths consume the same explicit threshold config and expose the effective threshold in operator-visible metadata.

findings.summary: OpenCode native mode bypasses the shared renderer, so ambiguity and prompt-boundary brief behavior can diverge from the shared hook contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Route OpenCode native mode through the shared render contract or explicitly port ambiguity and prompt-boundary invariants into the bridge helper.

findings.summary: Codex still uses a bespoke native fast path before the shared builder, so prompt policy, cache behavior, and shared payload semantics depend on transport/runtime branch.
findings.recommended_fix: Collapse Codex prompt-time routing onto buildSkillAdvisorBrief plus renderAdvisorBrief, or explicitly port prompt-policy, cache, deleted-skill, and shared-payload invariants into the fast path.

findings.summary: The MCP surface is asymmetric because advisor_status requires workspaceRoot while advisor_recommend and advisor_validate infer workspace implicitly from process state.
findings.recommended_fix: Normalize workspace selection across all public advisor tools by accepting explicit workspaceRoot everywhere and deprecating hidden cwd-only inference.

findings.summary: advisor_validate uses a 0.7 aggregate threshold for both corpus and holdout slices, while package docs describe stricter promotion gates and hook-time routing uses 0.8/0.35 prompt thresholds.
findings.recommended_fix: Publish explicit aggregate-vs-runtime threshold semantics, align validator thresholds with the documented promotion gate bundle where intended, and expose effective prompt-time thresholds in public outputs.

findings.summary: Hook telemetry is currently shaped as stderr emission plus an in-memory collector, so the shipped recommendation-health surface is ephemeral rather than durable.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a durable prompt-safe diagnostics sink and wire hook health summaries to a real operator-facing status surface instead of leaving them in-memory or stderr-only.

findings.summary: The public telemetry and validation surfaces have no accepted/corrected/ignored outcome signal, so recommendation quality still cannot be tuned from live routing behavior.
findings.recommended_fix: Define a minimal prompt-safe outcome event schema and connect it to advisor validation or a dedicated outcome-analysis surface.

findings.summary: advisor_status reports default laneWeights and the weights schema is literal-locked, so the public tool surface cannot represent live or candidate weight drift today.
findings.recommended_fix: Either report effective live/candidate weights through advisor_status and relax the literal-only schema, or narrow docs so laneWeights are clearly documented as static defaults.

findings.summary: Two-cycle promotion docs promise restart-persistent state through telemetry, but the implementation is still helper-plus-callback logic with no built-in durable store.
findings.recommended_fix: Either add an explicit durable persistence layer for promotion-cycle state or narrow the docs until restart durability is actually shipped.

findings.summary: Operator docs and playbooks still point to the old .opencode/plugins bridge path even though the live compat tests use .opencode/plugin-helpers.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the hook reference, feature catalog, and manual playbook to the plugin-helper bridge path and add a regression check that locks the documented bridge location.
